content:
Character: Dr. Thaddeus Pelagios
Perception: Thaddeus is short, round, white-haired, and dressed like a tenured explosion — corduroy, argyle, whale-tooth necklace, the works. He's my second-floor neighbor and occasional Sunday dinner companion. He's one of the smartest people I've ever met, even if half his theories make me want to pull out a whiteboard and show him why he's wrong. He argues like it's a sport and takes losing gracefully. I think he sees me as a worthy sparring partner. I know about the megalodon theory, the three expeditions, and that he keeps a live shark in his apartment, which I pretend doesn't concern me.
summary:
Character: Dr. Thaddeus Pelagios
Perception: Thaddeus is short, round, white-haired, and dressed like a tenured explosion — corduroy, argyle, whale-tooth necklace, the works. He's my second-floor neighbor and occasional Sunday dinner companion. He's one of the smartest people I've ever met, even if half his theories make me want to pull out a whiteboard and show him why he's wrong. He argues like it's a sport and takes losing gracefully. I think he sees me as a worthy sparring partner. I know about the megalodon theory, the three expeditions, and that he keeps a live shark in his apartment, which I pretend doesn't concern me.
